article_title: Intradural Extramedullary Spinal Tumor Suspected Angiosarcoma Based on Clinical Course and Pathological Findings: A Case Report
label: Figure 1.
caption: Preoperative MRIs.Sagittal T2-weighted (A), axial T1-weighted (B), and axial T2-weighted (C) MRIs showing a poorly marginated mass with a T1-low and T2-mosaic pattern located intradurally from T12 to L2 and compressing the spinal cord.

article_title: Intradural Extramedullary Spinal Tumor Suspected Angiosarcoma Based on Clinical Course and Pathological Findings: A Case Report
label: Figure 2.
caption: Intraoperative microscopic views.Intraoperative photographs following laminectomy and durotomy at T12–L2 showing a dark red mass in the subarachnoid space (A). The mass was not connected to the dura matter or spinal cord or cauda equina. After resection, no bleeding lesion or arteriovenous fistula are seen on the pia mater (B).

article_title: Intradural Extramedullary Spinal Tumor Suspected Angiosarcoma Based on Clinical Course and Pathological Findings: A Case Report
label: Figure 3.
caption: Histological findings.Hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining ×400 (A, B). Cluster of differentiation (CD) 31 staining ×200 (C), and CD34 staining ×200 (D).H&E staining showing atypical spindle cells in the stroma (black arrow). Proliferation of blood vessels is seen, but atypical cells are not seen in the vascular endothelium (white arrow). Cluster of differentiation (CD) 31 and 34 antigen staining is seen in vascular endothelial cells alone, but not stromal cells (C, D).

article_title: Intradural Extramedullary Spinal Tumor Suspected Angiosarcoma Based on Clinical Course and Pathological Findings: A Case Report
label: Figure 4.
caption: Postoperative MRIs.Sagittal T2-weighted MRI (A) at 2 weeks after surgery showing the mass was mostly resected, but a small mass is seen below the conus medullaris (white arrow).Sagittal T2-weighted MRI (B), and sagittal gadolinium-enhanced T1-weighted MRI (C) at 5 weeks after surgery showing an expanded mass and cerebrospinal fluid dissemination at C1 and L5 (black arrow).Sagittal T2-weighted MRI (D) after radiation therapy showing an expanded mass at the cervical spine.

article_title: Rare case of multiple neurofibromas of the scalp and trunk in association with intradural extramedullary spinal tumor: a case report
label: Figure 1
caption: Masses present all over the scalp, each averaging about 4×4 cm, with the largest one located in the occipital area of 6×7 cm in dimensions.

article_title: Rare case of multiple neurofibromas of the scalp and trunk in association with intradural extramedullary spinal tumor: a case report
label: Figure 2
caption: MRI of SPINE revealing T11–T12 neurofibroma.

article_title: Rare case of multiple neurofibromas of the scalp and trunk in association with intradural extramedullary spinal tumor: a case report
label: Figure 3
caption: Excised tumor by total excision from intraspinal region.

article_title: Intradural Disc Herniation at L5-S1 Mimicking an Intradural Extramedullary Spinal Tumor: A Case Report
label: Fig. 1
caption: T1-(A) and T2-weighted (B) sagittal magnetic resonance images demonstrating a mass-like lesion.

article_title: Intradural Disc Herniation at L5-S1 Mimicking an Intradural Extramedullary Spinal Tumor: A Case Report
label: Fig. 2
caption: Contrast-enhanced axial image showing peripheral enhancement of the lesion (arrow).

article_title: Intradural Disc Herniation at L5-S1 Mimicking an Intradural Extramedullary Spinal Tumor: A Case Report
label: Fig. 3
caption: Intraoperative photograph (A) outlining the peripheral displacement of the adherent cauda equine nerve roots (arrows) by the large intradural disc fragment. Intraoperative photograph (B) showing an anterior dural defect.
